title: Optics
subject: Physics
content:
# Optics

## Quick Synopsis

Ray optics uses geometry and sign conventions; wave optics uses interference, diffraction, and polarisation. JEE often mixes conceptual diagrams with formulas.

## Must Know

- Reflection and refraction follow Fermat-style path laws and Snell’s law.
- Spherical mirrors and lenses use consistent sign convention.
- Total internal reflection requires denser-to-rarer travel and angle greater than critical angle.
- Interference needs coherent sources with stable phase difference.
- Diffraction becomes significant when aperture size is comparable to wavelength.
- Polarisation proves transverse nature of light.

## Formulas / Rules

- Snell law: n1 sin i = n2 sin r.
- Mirror/lens formula: 1/f = 1/v + 1/u with sign convention.
- Lens maker: 1/f = (μ - 1)(1/R1 - 1/R2).
- Power of lens: P = 1/f in metre.
- YDSE fringe width: β = λD/d.
- Single slit minima: a sinθ = nλ.
- Brewster law: tan iB = μ.

## JEE Focus

- Draw ray diagrams before sign convention calculations.
- For lens combinations in contact, powers add.
- In YDSE, path difference determines bright/dark condition.
- For prism, minimum deviation formula links refractive index with A and δm.

## Common Traps

- Do not mix Cartesian and other sign conventions.
- Virtual image distance signs depend on chosen convention.
- Coherence is necessary for sustained interference.
- Fringe width is independent of fringe order in ideal YDSE.

## 10-Minute Revision Checklist

- Can you solve mirror/lens image position problems?
- Can you apply total internal reflection?
- Can you calculate YDSE fringe width and shift?
- Can you identify diffraction minima?
